title: Congressional elections
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on congressional elections, the incumbency advantage, primaries, caucuses, and general elections
topic_path: How are members of Congress elected?

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below for the following question. 
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40c8e11a0b5462030f7f81419eabc73b.png)
Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**Which of the following accurately compares the presidential and midterm election trends from 1972 to 2014?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Presidential election turnout has increased, while midterm election turnout has remained relatively constant at lower percentages
Neither the presidential nor the midterm election turnouts have broken the 60 percent participation barrier in the past 40 years
Both the presidential and midterm election turnouts remain largely constant and unchanged
Presidential election turnout remains largely unchanged, while midterm election turnout has declined rapidly

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below for the following question. 
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40c8e11a0b5462030f7f81419eabc73b.png)
Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**If a campaign strategist in 2017 were projecting turnout for the 2018 midterms, which of the following would be the best prediction based on the data presented in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Midterm turnout in 2018 is likely to rise to the high 30s or low 40s
Midterm turnout in 2018 will remain largely unchanged from 2014
Midterm turnout in 2018 should overtake the presidential turnout in 2016
Midterm turnout in 2018 should decrease dramatically to the low 30s

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below for the following question. 
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40c8e11a0b5462030f7f81419eabc73b.png)
Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**Based on the data presented in the graph, which of the following is the best conclusion one can make about the significance of presidential and midterm election turnout rates in this period?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: With lower overall turnout in midterm elections, groups that are more likely to vote had more impact on their outcomes
The party of the president increasingly earned congressional seats during midterm elections
Congressional seats became more competitive based on the trend in midterm election turnout
Because presidential elections have higher participation levels, presidential candidates catered more to issues that matter to voters of all races

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the graph below for the following question. 
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40c8e11a0b5462030f7f81419eabc73b.png)
Source: [United States Election Project](http://www.electproject.org/national-1789-present)

**Which of the following statements is supported by the data shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Voters turn out at a higher rate for presidential elections than in midterm elections
Voters turn out at a lower rate for presidential elections than in midterm elections
Voters turn out at generally the same rate for both general elections and midterm elections
Voter turnout has increased for midterm elections but decreased for presidential elections from 2010 to 2014

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Immediately after they shall be assembled in Consequence of the first Election, they shall be divided as equally as may be into three Classes.”

-Article I, Section 3, Clause 2 of the United States Constitution

**What is the constitutional purpose of assigning US senators to one of three classes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: By gradually changing membership, rotations encourage deliberate debate and leadership continuity after major elections
Senators are assigned to one of three classes based on their seniority and prior experience in other elected offices
Senators are assigned a class to determine which committees they will chair during their term of office
Class assignment creates a smaller cadre of legislators to be trained in how to apply the chamber rules of debate

assessment_items.question_content:
**2016 Election Cycle: House of Representatives**

Type of Candidate | Total Raised | Number of Candidates | Average Raised
- | - | - | -
**Incumbent** | \$664,873,622 | 418 | \$1,590,607
**Challenger** | \$149,000,679 | 643 | \$231,727
**Open Seat** | \$214,460,277  | 337 | \$636,381
**Grand Total** | \$1,028,334,578 | 1,398 | \$735,576
Source: [OpenSecrets.org](https://www.opensecrets.org/overview/incumbs.php?cycle=2016)


 **Based on this election spending table for House races in 2016, which is the best conclusion one can make about the significance of this data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Incumbent House members have an overwhelming advantage over challengers
Open seat elections give incumbents an advantage regardless of the amount of campaign funds raised
House members facing opposition are less likely to be reelected than senators
House members must start working on their next campaign soon after taking office in DC

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

**2012 Pennsylvania congressional districts by party**
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8d18b8e1cc5e762d26b39fea620aabc3.png)

Source:  [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2012_Pennsylvania_congressional_districts_by_party.png)

The 2012 redistricting process in Pennsylvania resulted in the map shown above, even though Democratic candidates won 50.5% of the total votes cast in the 2012 congressional elections.

**Which of the following terms best applies to this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Partisan gerrymandering
Free rider problem
Incumbency advantage
Apportionment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement accurately summarizes how constituencies affect the campaign strategy of candidates running for the US Senate or House of Representatives?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Candidates for House districts must consider specific needs and critical issues that characterize a relatively small number of voters
Candidates for a House seat must consider issues and needs that matter to neighboring districts in order to be elected
Senate candidates must address issues that the president thinks are important to satisfy constituents
Senatorial campaign strategies must consider issues affecting a narrower range of ideologies and needs than those found in House districts

assessment_items.question_content:
>"The House of Representatives shall be composed of Members chosen every second Year by the People of the several States . . ."

-Article 1, Section 2 of the United States Constitution

**Which of the following statements best explains the Framers’ rationale for this constitutional provision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All members of the House must stand for reelection every two years in order to better serve the immediate needs of constituents in their districts
House members were usually not as well off as their Senate colleagues, so they needed to return home more regularly to provide for their families
Because the Framers knew that members of the House did not live very long, it was better to replace them with younger candidates every two years
House members are prone to undue influence from constituents in their district, so their terms were shortened to prevent long-term damage to the republic

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains why direct primaries have become a mainstay of American politics since the early 1900s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The democratic merits of giving the electorate more influence in choosing candidates outweigh the benefits of depending on party leaders' expertise
Major parties are tired of seeing their national candidates beaten in the general election by third-party and independent candidates
Party leaders have become notorious for choosing candidates who are untrained and not well known by the voters
Direct primaries ensure that party candidates will toe the line regarding their party’s platform

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of an open primary and closed primary?**

 | Open primary | Closed primary
- | - | -
**A** | Only used for congressional elections | Only used for presidential elections 
**B** | Party members choose nominees after hours of speeches and debates | Meeting of delegates from one political party to select party candidates for public office
**C** | Voters registered with another party or as independents can vote |Registered independents can vote along with members of a registered party
**D** | Any person can participate in this election | Limited to registered members of a political party

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2cf649125af8c16bde5bae91d84d114.png)

**Which of the following is an accurate statement about the information in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Members of the House typically had higher incumbency rates than Senators in this period
Senators were more likely to be reelected than members of the House of Representatives in this period
The Senate incumbent reelection rate decreased over time as people became more dissatisfied with Congress
Members of the House were more likely to face political challengers in their primary elections in this period.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2cf649125af8c16bde5bae91d84d114.png)

**Based on the information in the graph, which of the following statements is true about congressional elections over this period?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The majority of incumbents in both houses won their races in every election
Incumbents were more likely to win elections in the Senate, but not the House
The majority of incumbents in both houses were less likely to win elections because they didn’t have enough time to appropriately finance their campaigns
Incumbents were less likely to win elections because of critiques of their policy decisions while in office
